questions.scenario: You're at 2 cartridges. Solid Barrel combo ready.
questions.question: What happens after Solid Barrel?
questions.explanation: Solid Barrel generates 1 cartridge. At 2, you'll go to 3 (max). Don't Solid Barrel at 3 cartridges.

questions.scenario: You have 1 cartridge. No Mercy isn't available.
questions.question: Should you use Burst Strike?
questions.explanation: Generally save cartridges for No Mercy windows. Use Burst Strike only to prevent overcapping.

questions.scenario: Combat starting. Cartridge gauge empty.
questions.question: How do you build cartridges?
questions.explanation: Cartridges come from Solid Barrel (1) and Bloodfest (3). Only combo finisher generates gauge.

questions.scenario: Burst Strike used. Hypervelocity is now available.
questions.question: What is Hypervelocity?
questions.explanation: Burst Strike grants Hypervelocity (Ready to Blast), a free oGCD follow-up attack.

questions.scenario: 3 cartridges. Gnashing Fang costs 1, Double Down costs 2.
questions.question: Which should you use first in burst?
questions.explanation: Double Down is your highest damage cartridge spender. Use it early in No Mercy for maximum value.

questions.scenario: Tankbuster incoming. Heart of Corundum available.
questions.question: What does Heart of Corundum provide?
questions.explanation: Heart of Corundum provides 15% mitigation, a heal when damaged, and a shield. Extremely powerful.

questions.scenario: Tank autos hurting. Aurora has 2 charges.
questions.question: What does Aurora provide?
questions.explanation: Aurora is a HoT (heal over time). Use it freely with 2 charges to smooth out damage.

questions.scenario: Big tankbuster. Nebula and Heart of Corundum available.
questions.question: What's the best combination?
questions.explanation: For big tankbusters, stack Nebula (30%) with Heart of Corundum (15% + heal + shield).

questions.scenario: Sustained auto-attacks. Camouflage available.
questions.question: What makes Camouflage good for autos?
questions.explanation: Camouflage boosts parry rate, which is particularly effective against frequent auto-attacks.

questions.scenario: You used Rampart. Another hit coming soon.
questions.question: What pairs well with active Rampart?
questions.explanation: Heart of Corundum (25s CD) pairs well with Rampart (90s CD). Save Nebula for when Rampart is down.

questions.scenario: You're at 80% HP. Superbolide available.
questions.question: What happens when you use Superbolide?
questions.explanation: Superbolide drops you to 1 HP immediately, then makes you invulnerable for 10 seconds.

questions.scenario: Superbolide has 360 second cooldown (6 minutes).
questions.question: How does this compare to other tank invulns?
questions.explanation: Superbolide (360s) is second shortest after Holmgang (240s). Can be used fairly often.

questions.scenario: You're at 20% HP. Lethal hit coming.
questions.question: Is Superbolide worth using here?
questions.explanation: At 20% with a lethal hit coming, Superbolide saves you. You drop to 1 HP but don't die.

questions.scenario: Superbolide used. You're at 1 HP with 10s invuln.
questions.question: What should healers do?
questions.explanation: You're invuln for 10s but at 1 HP. Healers should heal you during the invuln window so you're stable when it ends.

questions.scenario: Big pull in dungeon. About to die.
questions.question: Is Superbolide good here?
questions.explanation: Superbolide works great in dungeons for big pulls. 10 seconds of invulnerability lets you and healer stabilize.

questions.scenario: No Mercy coming off CD. You have 1 cartridge.
questions.question: What should you do before No Mercy?
questions.explanation: Enter No Mercy with 2+ cartridges so you can use Double Down (costs 2). Build gauge first.

questions.scenario: No Mercy active. 3 cartridges available.
questions.question: What should you use first?
questions.explanation: Double Down is your highest potency attack. Use it early in No Mercy to guarantee it lands in the window.

questions.scenario: Bloodfest is ready. You have 0 cartridges.
questions.question: What does Bloodfest provide?
questions.explanation: Bloodfest instantly grants 3 cartridges. Use it during No Mercy when you've spent your cartridges.

questions.scenario: No Mercy has 5 seconds left. Gnashing Fang available.
questions.question: Should you start Gnashing Fang?
questions.explanation: Gnashing Fang combo is fast. 5 seconds is enough time to complete it within No Mercy.

questions.scenario: No Mercy provides 20% damage boost for 20 seconds.
questions.question: What should you pack into this window?
questions.explanation: Pack all your big damage into No Mercy: Double Down, Gnashing Fang combo, Sonic Break, oGCDs.

questions.scenario: Gnashing Fang ready. You have 1 cartridge.
questions.question: What does Gnashing Fang start?
questions.explanation: Gnashing Fang starts a 3-hit combo: Gnashing Fang > Savage Claw > Wicked Talon, each with a Continuation follow-up.

questions.scenario: You just used Gnashing Fang. Jugular Rip is available.
questions.question: What is Jugular Rip?
questions.explanation: Jugular Rip is the Continuation oGCD after Gnashing Fang. Weave it before your next GCD.

questions.scenario: Full Gnashing Fang combo with Continuations.
questions.question: What's the complete sequence?
options: GF > JR > SC > AT > WT > EG
questions.explanation: Gnashing Fang > Jugular Rip > Savage Claw > Abdomen Tear > Wicked Talon > Eye Gouge. GCD > oGCD pattern.

questions.scenario: You used Savage Claw but forgot Abdomen Tear.
questions.question: Can you still use Abdomen Tear?
questions.explanation: Each Continuation must be used before the next GCD. Miss it and it's gone. Practice the rhythm.

questions.scenario: Gnashing Fang is on 30 second cooldown.
questions.question: How often can you do this combo?
questions.explanation: 30 second cooldown means you can do Gnashing Fang combo twice per minute, aligning with No Mercy windows.

questions.scenario: Magic raidwide incoming. Heart of Light available.
questions.question: What does Heart of Light provide?
questions.explanation: Heart of Light provides 10% magic damage mitigation to the entire party for 15 seconds.

questions.scenario: Party member about to take big damage.
questions.question: Can you use Heart of Corundum on them?
questions.explanation: Heart of Corundum can target any party member, giving them the mitigation, heal, and shield.

questions.scenario: Physical raidwide coming. Heart of Light available.
questions.question: Should you use Heart of Light?
questions.explanation: Heart of Light only mitigates magic damage. For physical raidwides, use Reprisal instead.

questions.scenario: Healer taking damage. Aurora available.
questions.question: Can you Aurora the healer?
questions.explanation: Aurora can be placed on any party member. Use it on struggling healers or DPS taking damage.

questions.scenario: Co-tank about to take a tankbuster. Your Heart of Corundum ready.
questions.question: Should you use it on them?
questions.explanation: Heart of Corundum on the co-tank stacks with their own mitigation, ensuring survival on big hits.

questions.scenario: Sonic Break ready. No Mercy active.
questions.question: Why use Sonic Break in No Mercy?
questions.explanation: Sonic Break applies a DoT. Using it during No Mercy means the DoT ticks also get the 20% damage buff.

questions.scenario: Multiple enemies. Bow Shock available.
questions.question: What makes Bow Shock good for AoE?
questions.explanation: Bow Shock applies a DoT to all targets hit. In AoE, this is significant damage over time.

questions.scenario: Blasting Zone off cooldown. No burst window.
questions.question: Should you use Blasting Zone?
questions.explanation: Blasting Zone is a 30 second CD oGCD. Use it on cooldown - don't lose uses waiting for burst.

questions.scenario: No Mercy grants access to Reign of Beasts.
questions.question: What is Reign of Beasts?
questions.explanation: Reign of Beasts is a new 3-hit combo granted during No Mercy. Another burst combo to fit in the window.

questions.scenario: Tank swap needed. You have No Mercy coming up.
questions.question: What's the optimal order?
questions.explanation: Provoke first to get the boss, then use No Mercy. You want to be actively attacking during your burst.
